title: Accessing Shared Network Drives and File Shares
text:
Q: How do I access shared network drives and file shares?
A: Ensure you are connected to the company network (Ethernet or VPN if working remotely), then follow the steps for your operating system.

Windows: Step 1: Open File Explorer and click 'This PC' in the left sidebar. Step 2: Click the 'Computer' tab at the top and select 'Map network drive'. Step 3: Choose a drive letter from the dropdown. Step 4: In the 'Folder' field, enter the path in the format \\servername\sharename (replace with the actual server and share names provided by IT). Step 5: Check 'Reconnect at sign-in' if you want the drive mapped automatically on login. Step 6: Click Finish and enter your network credentials (username: domain\yourusername) when prompted. The shared drive will appear under 'This PC'.

macOS: Step 1: Open Finder and press Cmd+K (or go to Go > Connect to Server). Step 2: Enter the server address in the format smb://servername/sharename and click Connect. Step 3: Enter your network credentials when prompted. Step 4: The shared drive will appear on your Desktop and under Locations in the Finder sidebar. To reconnect at login, add it to Login Items in System Settings > General > Login Items.

If you receive an 'Access Denied' error, contact IT to verify your account has permission for that share.
